summary.intent: Fix a Chrome/Brave login bug reported in GitHub issue #370 where login fails on Chromium-based browsers but works on Safari/Firefox.
summary.outcome: Added the `Secure` attribute to session cookies when TLS is active or behind a proxy, fixing Chrome's strict cookie enforcement. All 16 auth_routes tests pass and the fix was committed, pushed, and a PR was created.

repo: GatewayState has a `tls_active: bool` field used to detect HTTPS connections
AuthState contains `gateway_state: Arc<GatewayState>` providing access to TLS and proxy settings
`behind_proxy` implies TLS termination at the proxy level, so `tls_active || behind_proxy` is the correct `secure` signal
There are 6 cookie construction sites in auth_routes.rs: session_response(), clear_session_response(), and an inline cookie in the vault recovery key path
The project uses beads issues for tracking fixes

code.finding: Inline cookie construction for vault/setup first-run path that also needed the Secure flag added — easy to miss since it's not going through session_response()
session_response() and clear_session_response() are called at lines 333, 353, 813, 955 — all needed updating to pass the secure parameter

workflow: Chrome strictly requires the Secure attribute on cookies from HTTPS responses; Safari and Firefox are more lenient — this is a common source of environment-specific auth bugs
Building and running targeted test suites (cargo test -p gateway) before committing is a reliable verification step for Rust workspace projects

friction: Multiple call sites for session_response() and clear_session_response() required multiple read/edit cycles to locate and update — no single grep captured all of them at once
The inline cookie at line 254 was a non-obvious second code path not covered by the helper functions, requiring careful full-file review
open_items: A beads issue was created for the fix but the PR details (URL, number) are not captured in the transcript
Tests added cover TLS-active and proxy cases but there are no tests for the inline vault recovery cookie path specifically
